智利北部的阿塔卡马沙漠是地球上最干旱的地方之一-多年来，这里根本没有降雨。为了使农业社区生存和发展，他们将需要水和土壤养分，两者都供不应求。
 然而，人们早在现代技术出现之前就住在阿塔卡马。通过使用绿洲的水和复杂的灌溉系统解决了缺水问题。对于土壤养分，他们遇到的解决方案是在1450年左右印加人到达之前的几个世纪，他们以海鸟粪便或“鸟粪”的形式从海岸引进了超级肥料。
  这是我们新研究的关键发现，其中我们分析了在阿塔卡马（Atacama）的14个考古遗址发现的246种作物和野生植物的残留。这些植物覆盖了近3,000年的历史，涵盖了多个古代文明时期，其次是印加，最后是欧洲殖民时期，直至1800年。
 判断鸟粪是否曾用于这些古老植物的肥料的一种方法是寻找植物残骸中氮同位素（15N / 14N）的比率。这两种同位素的原子质量不同，因此它们在自然系统中的行为略有不同，因此可以充当自然生物化学过程的示踪剂。我们知道，即使少量的海鸟鸟粪化肥也会对现代玉米中的氮同位素比率产生巨大影响，使氮同位素比率远远高于自然或使用任何其他肥料时的氮同位素比率。当我们查看包括玉米，南瓜和辣椒在内的考古作物遗骸时，我们发现类似的高同位素比可追溯到公元1000年左右。
 在该地区人类骨骼中发现的氮同位素比在干旱条件下保存完好，与作物平行变化也很大。科学家们早些时候以为，这表明人们已经从海里吞下了鱼。众所周知，由于洪堡洋流非常寒冷且营养丰富，海水鱼类的氮同位素比很高，尤其是智利沿海的鱼类。我们的研究发现，远古的阿塔卡马地区的人们确实从鱼类中获得了较高的氮同位素值，只是它是间接地通过海鸟进食而将其作为鸟粪排泄而来的，而鸟粪则将其排泄为鸟粪，从而成为农作物的肥料。
   我们的研究还发现，并非所有人似乎都可以使用这种超级肥料。尽管有迹象表明从AD1000开始，玉米粒中的高氮同位素比显着增加，这表明作物产量有了相当大的提高，并允许更大的定居点，但有些粒缺乏这一证据。相反，他们表现出其他肥料的迹象，例如绿色堆肥或来自骆马及其亲属的粪便。
  来自同一公墓和同一时期的骨骼也显示出其氮同位素比率的巨大差异，这表明获得肥料的农作物在整个社区中分布不均。可能有些家庭或氏族享有与海岸（相距约90公里）的联系的特权，并能够获得海鸟鸟粪，并将其主要用作自身的优势，以此作为力量和声望的来源。沙漠开花了-但对于其他人来说，更多。 
鸟粪受精一直持续到印加和殖民时期。 在19世纪初期，它在世界范围内以超级肥料而闻名，并成为秘鲁（阿塔卡马州当时的一部分）的重要收入来源。 每年有数十万吨的产品被运往海外，主要运往美国和西欧，而此时鸟粪被称为“白金”。  不久之后，首先通过一种新的阿塔卡马沙漠资源-硝石（硝酸钠）进行了广泛开采，从而削弱了鸟粪的重要性，然后在20世纪初引入了廉价的合成肥料。 鸟粪肥料已经通过在有机农业中使用而重新流行，但是在智利，现在禁止提取最近的鸟粪，而且鸟粪和生产它的海鸟均受到法律保护。
note_en:
The Atacama Desert of northern Chile is one of the driest places on the planet – in many years it receives no rain whatsoever. For farming communities to survive and thrive they would need water and soil nutrients, both in short supply.
 Yet people did live in the Atacama, long before modern technology. The water shortage was addressed using water from oases and complex irrigation systems. For soil nutrients, the solution they hit upon – centuries before the arrival of the Inca in around 1450 – was to bring a super-fertiliser from the coast in the form of seabird excrement, or “guano”.
  This was the key finding of our  new research in which we analysed the remains of 246 crop and wild plants found in 14 archaeological sites in the Atacama. These plants cover a nearly 3,000 year period spanning several ancient civilisations, followed by the Inca, and finally the period of European colonisation up to 1800.
 One way to tell if guano was used to fertilise these ancient plants is to look for the ratios of the isotopes of nitrogen (15N/14N) in the plant remains. These two isotopes differ only in atomic mass, but as a result they behave a little differently in natural systems and so can act as tracers of natural, biochemical processes. We know that even small amounts of seabird guano fertiliser has a  massive impact on the nitrogen isotope ratios in modern maize, raising them far above what is possible either naturally or using any other fertiliser. When we looked at archaeological crop remains, including maize, squash and chilli peppers, we found similarly high isotope ratios in plants dating from around AD 1000 and onwards.
 Nitrogen isotope ratios found in human skeletons from the region, well-preserved in the arid conditions, also changed dramatically in parallel with the crops. Scientists had earlier thought that this showed that people had eaten fish from the sea. Marine fish are known to have high nitrogen isotope ratios, especially those off the coast of Chile thanks to the very cold and nutrient-rich waters of the  Humboldt Current. Our research found that people in ancient Atacama communities did obtain those high nitrogen isotope values from fish – except that it was indirectly, via seabirds who ate the fish and then excreted it as guano, which became fertiliser for crops.
   Our research also found that not everyone seems to have had access to this super-fertiliser. While there were signs that high nitrogen isotope ratios increased markedly in maize kernels from AD1000 onwards, indicating a considerable increase in crop yields and allowing larger settlements, some kernels lacked this evidence. Rather they showed signs of other fertilisers such as green compost or dung from llamas and their relatives.
  Skeletons from the same cemeteries and dating to the same period also showed dramatic differences in their nitrogen isotope ratios, suggesting that access to manured crops was not evenly distributed through the community. It might be that some families or clans had privileged links with the coast (some 90km distant) and were able to obtain seabird guano and use it primarily for their own advantage as a source of power and prestige. The desert bloomed – but for some more than for others.
  Guano fertilisation continued into the Inca and colonial periods. In the early 19th century, it became more widely known worldwide as a super-fertiliser and became a significant source of income for Peru (which the Atacama was then part of). Hundreds of thousands of tonnes were shipped overseas each year, mainly to the US and western Europe – it was at this time that guano became known as “white gold”.
 Guano’s importance was eclipsed shortly afterwards firstly by a new Atacama Desert resource, saltpetre (sodium nitrate), which was  extensively mined, and then by the introduction of cheaper synthetic fertilisers in the early 20th century. Guano fertiliser has undergone resurgence through its use in organic farming, but in Chile it is now forbidden to extract recent guano, and both the guano and the seabirds that produce it are protected by law.
title: 海鸟便便使印加前文明在阿塔卡马沙漠蓬勃发展
title_en: A pre-Incan civilisation thrived in the Atacama Desert thanks to seabird poo

在德克萨斯州南部，星期四下午和星期五早上又有一些戏剧性的场面。 SpaceX连续两天撤离了留在Boca Chica村的少数居民。警长代表清理海滩和封闭道路。在该公司的发射场，一艘星舰火箭原型机正在进行发射准备工作。
 车辆已准备就绪，地面设备正在排气。风很轻。然后，什么都没有。随着时间的流逝，火箭留在了地面上。不幸的是，对于SpaceX，美国联邦航空管理局没有最终批准发射。这一切都导致了南德克萨斯州的一场对决。
  SpaceX正式表示其目标是周四的发射尝试，但尚未就发生的事情发表评论。除了提供通用声明外，FAA也没有提供具体细节：“我们将继续与SpaceX合作，解决未决的安全问题，然后再批准下一次试飞。”
 尽管我们不知道为什么没有批准的确切细节，但了解FAA在监管发射许可证方面试图做什么以及SpaceX必须做什么才能有所帮助。这使我们能够就什么原因阻止SN9的发布，现在不早于星期一。为了汇总此常见问题，Ars与任务保证和FAA规则专家进行了交谈。
  它是有权授予美国公司商业空间发射许可的联邦机构。 FAA不太关心任务目标本身，而更关心保护发射场附近以及上方空域的生命和财产。底线：除非有引起公众安全的担忧，否则联邦航空局不会推迟飞行测试。
     每次发射实验性飞船时，SpaceX必须事先获得美国联邦航空局（FAA）的批准。联邦法规（Code of Federal Regulations）的一系列部分和规则明确阐明了这样做的规则。 SpaceX的任务是说服FAA这款飞机值得飞行，他们已经建立了足够大的安全通道并发出了适当的通知。该代码的相关部分是431.31，该文件说，FAA安全审查将确定公司的车辆是否可以在不危害公众健康和安全以及财产安全的情况下发射和降落。通常在飞行48小时内会进行“发射准备情况审查”，以证明这一点。
  根据规定，如果FAA拒绝了RLV任务执照申请的安全批准，则FAA书面通知申请人。该通知说明了FAA做出决定的原因。申请人可以对做出决定的原因做出回应，并要求重新考虑。＆＃34;看来我们现在正在SpaceX获得重新审议的过程中。 
是的，美国联邦航空局已经发布了SpaceX的Boca Chica发射场的空域限制（请参阅此处的最新列表）。但是，这些限制并不表示已获美国联邦航空局（FAA）批准，而是警告一定时间空域可能被封锁。 （对于水手而言，NOTAMS与此类似）。
  互联网上没有任何地方可以搜索特定任务的飞船发射批准。因此，只有当SpaceX公开它或实际发生发射时，它才成为公共知识。
  尽管SN9的高空飞行剖面与SN8的飞行相似，但预计仅会延伸10公里，而不是12.5公里。特定于任务的发射批准必须考虑到详细信息中的每项更改，需要进行新的分析和更新的安全通道。 SN8飞行中可能发生了一些事情，引起了FAA的注意并引起了更多的审查。 SN8和SN9的航班之间也可能出现重大问题。
     如果不知道SpaceX的意图，就不可能回答这个问题。该公司可能只是在进行湿衣服彩排测试。但是，诸如推进剂装载或推特上的埃隆·马斯克（Elon Musk）沮丧之类的任何恐吓都不太可能在FAA商业太空运输副署长Wayne Monteith身上起作用。他是一位受人尊敬的官员，不被推文影响。
   不可以。FAA的利益在于许可和促进商业太空活动。但是，联邦机构与越来越多的寻求许可证的公司合作过度。美国联邦航空局（FAA）可能会为在南德克萨斯州（Star Texas）推出的星舰（Starship）推出更多资源，以解决其余问题，从而使公司飞行。
  大概。有许多公司试图提供响应空间，而FAA则试图简化许可流程。他们正在寻找人员来增强他们审查启动应用程序的能力。美国联邦航空局（FAA）通过发布有关发射和再入许可要求的最终规则，朝着提高响应速度迈出了一步。但这是一个持续的过程。
  SpaceX最近确实获得了两个非常大型的海上平台，用于从中发射和降落其星际飞船。但是，它并没有这样做来避开FAA许可。与SeaLaunch相似，该公司仍将按照FAA规定运营。从监管的角度来看，试图在国际水域发射，可能会使SpaceX变得更加困难。 
嗯，不，公司不会流氓。 除了获得商业发射许可外，FAA还对SpaceX在南德克萨斯州的设施进行的环境审查，以确定其是否适合星舰和超重型发射。  冷静的头脑很可能占上风，SpaceX和FAA在星期五和周末一起工作以确保所需的权限。 也许马斯克会邀请Monteith从该公司位于德克萨斯州南部的任务控制设施查看发射情况。 无论如何，这都不是Musk或Monteith的第一个牛仔竞技表演，聪明的钱正在他们身上解决。
note_en:
There were moments of high drama on Thursday afternoon, and again Friday morning, in South Texas. For two days in a row, SpaceX evacuated the handful of residents remaining in Boca Chica Village. Sheriff's deputies cleared beaches and closed roads. And at the company's launch site, a Starship rocket prototype underwent preparations for launch.
 The vehicle was ready, with ground equipment venting away. The winds were light. And then—nothing. As the hours ticked by, the rocket remained on the ground. Unfortunately for SpaceX, the Federal Aviation Administration had not given its final approval to launch. It all made for quite a South Texas Showdown.
  SpaceX, which officially said it was targeting a launch attempt on Thursday, has not publicly commented on what happened. Nor has the FAA provided specifics on what transpired, other than  offering a generic statement: “We will continue working with SpaceX to resolve outstanding safety issues before we approve the next test flight.”
 Although we don't know the precise details why an approval did not come, it is helpful to understand what the FAA is trying to do in terms of regulating launch licenses, and what SpaceX must do to comply. This allows us to make some informed guesses about what is holding up the launch of SN9, which will  now occur no earlier than Monday. To put together this FAQ, Ars spoke with experts in mission assurance and FAA rules.
  It is the federal agency with the authority to license commercial space launches by US companies. The FAA is less concerned with the mission goals themselves, and more so protecting the lives and property near the launch site, as well as the air space above. Bottom line: t he FAA would not delay a flight test unless there was a cause for public safety concern.
     For each launch of its experimental Starship vehicle, SpaceX must obtain approval from the FAA in advance. The rules for doing so are clearly spelled out  in the Code of Federal Regulations, in a series of parts and rules. It is the job of SpaceX to convince the FAA its vehicle is worthy of flight, that they have established a large enough safety corridor, and made the proper notifications. The relevant part of the code is  431.31, which says the FAA safety review will determine whether a company's vehicle can launch and land "without jeopardizing public health and safety and the safety of property." There is typically a Launch Readiness Review within 48 hours of flight to prove this.
  According to the regulations, "The FAA notifies an applicant, in writing, if the FAA has denied safety approval for an RLV mission license application. The notice states the reasons for the FAA's determination. The applicant may respond to the reasons for the determination and request reconsideration." It seems likely that we are now in the process of SpaceX obtaining reconsideration.
  Yes, the FAA had posted air space restrictions ( see here for a current list) for SpaceX's Boca Chica launch site. However, these restrictions are not indicative of FAA approval, but rather a warning that air space may be locked out at a certain time. (It is similar for  NOTAMs for mariners).
  There is nowhere on the Internet to search for a mission-specific Starship launch approval. So it becomes public knowledge only if SpaceX discloses it, or if a launch actually happens.
  While the high-altitude flight profile for SN9 is similar to the  flight made by SN8, it is expected to go only to 10km instead of 12.5km. A mission-specific launch approval must account for every change in details, requiring new analyses, and an updated safety corridor. Perhaps something happened during the SN8 flight that captured the FAA's attention and provoked extra scrutiny. It is also possible that a significant issue arose between the flights of SN8 and SN9.
     Without knowing SpaceX's intentions, it's impossible to answer this. The company may simply have been conducting a wet-dress rehearsal test. But any intimidation such as propellant loading, or Elon Musk's  frustration on Twitter, would be unlikely to work on  Wayne Monteith, the FAA Associate Administrator for Commercial Space Transportation. He is a well regarded official, not known to be influenced by tweets.
   No. It's in the FAA's interest to license and promote commercial space activities. However, the federal agency is overworked with an increasing number of companies seeking licenses. It's likely that the FAA will be bringing additional resources to bear on the remaining issues with the Starship launch in South Texas to try and get the company flying.
  Probably. There are a lot of companies trying to do responsive space, and the FAA is trying to streamline the licensing process. They're looking to staff up to increase their capability to review launch applications.  The FAA took a step toward being more responsive in December,  by issuing a final rule on launch and reentry licensing requirements. But this is an ongoing process.
  SpaceX did recently acquire two very large offshore platforms to launch and land its Starship vehicle from. However, it did not do this to skirt FAA licensing. Similar to SeaLaunch, the company would still operate under FAA regulations. Trying to launch in international waters, if anything, would probably make it more difficult for SpaceX from a regulatory standpoint.
  Uhh, no, the company is not going to go rogue. And in addition to licensing commercial launches, the FAA is also  conducting an environmental review of SpaceX's facility in South Texas for its suitability for Starship and Super Heavy launches.
  It's likely that cooler heads prevail, and SpaceX and the FAA work together on Friday and over the weekend to secure needed permissions. Perhaps Musk will invite Monteith to view the launch from the company's mission control facility in South Texas. In any case, this is neither the first rodeo for Musk or Monteith, and the smart money is on them working this out.
title: 伊隆·马斯克（Elon Musk），联邦航空局（FAA）和星舰（Starship）到底发生了什么？
title_en: What’s really going on with Elon Musk, the FAA, and Starship?

基于使用的模型允许客户以较低的成本启动，从而最大程度地减少了入门方面的磨擦，同时仍保留了随着时间的推移从客户获利的能力，因为价格与客户获得的价值直接相关。客户可以发现新的用例，而不限制可以访问该软件的用户数量，这可以带来更多的长期成功和更高的生命周期价值。
 虽然我们不会在一夜之间就100％基于使用情况进行研究，但要查看软件的一些大趋势-自动化，人工智能和API-产品的价值通常不会随着登录次数的增加而增加。基于使用量的定价将是未来成功获利的关键。这里有四个主要技巧，可以帮助公司通过这种模式将ARR扩大到100+百万美元。
  基于使用量的定价在技术堆栈的所有层中。尽管它是基础架构层的先驱（例如：AWS和Azure），但在基于API的产品和应用程序软件中，它在基础架构，中间件和应用程序中越来越受欢迎。
  一些人担心投资者会讨厌基于使用量的定价，因为客户没有被锁定在订阅中。但是，投资者实际上将其视为客户从产品中看到价值的标志，并且没有货架。
 实际上，投资者越来越在市场上奖励基于使用情况的公司。基于使用情况的公司的交易收入是其同行的50％的倍数溢价。
 投资者尤其喜欢基于使用量的定价模型与土地和土地经营模型相结合的方式。在过去三年的首次公开募股中，九家净美元保留率最高的公司中有七家采用了基于使用情况的模型。尤其是雪花，其净美元保留率为158％。
note_en:
The usage-based model allows a customer to start at a low cost, minimizing friction to getting started while still preserving the ability to monetize a customer over time because the price is directly tied with the value a customer receives. Not limiting the number of users who can access the software, customers are able to find new use cases — which leads to more long-term success and higher lifetime value.
 While we aren’t going 100% usage-based overnight, looking at some of the megatrends in software —  automation, AI and APIs — the value of a product normally doesn’t scale with more logins. Usage-based pricing will be the key to successful monetization in the future. Here are four top tips to help companies scale to $100+ million ARR with this model.
  Usage-based pricing is in all layers of the tech stack. Though it was pioneered in the infrastructure layer (think: AWS and Azure), it’s becoming increasingly popular for API-based products and application software — across infrastructure, middleware and applications.
  Some fear that investors will hate usage-based pricing because customers aren’t locked into a subscription. But, investors actually see it as a sign that customers are seeing value from a product and there’s no shelf-ware.
 In fact, investors are increasingly rewarding usage-based companies in the market. Usage-based companies are trading at a 50% revenue multiple premium over their peers.
 Investors especially love how the usage-based pricing model pairs with the land-and-expand business model. And of the IPOs over the last three years, seven of the nine that had the best net dollar retention all have a usage-based model. Snowflake in particular is off the charts with a 158% net dollar retention.
title: 基于订阅的定价已死：智能SaaS公司正在转向基于使用的模型
title_en: Subscription-based pricing is dead: Smart SaaS companies are shifting to usage-based models
